description: A composite designed graphic combining a photo of President Trump with a data chart and a DOL seal. The right two-thirds is a white horizontal bar chart titled in bold navy 'Prices for Many Goods and Services are Falling' with the italic subhead 'Year-over-year price change'. The chart lists ~24 categories each with a navy bar extending leftward and a percent label, all negative: Eggs -39.2%, Smartphones -12.4%, Sports tickets -10.0%, Men's suits -7.1%, Health insurance -6.1%, School supplies -6.0%, Butter -5.8%, Margarine -4.4%, Tax prep -4.1%, College textbooks -3.9%, Cell phone plans -3.7%, Financial services -3.7%, Cheese -3.1%, Potatoes -3.0%, Laundry equipment -2.8%, Flour -2.8%, Used cars & trucks -2.7%, Baby food & formula -2.3%, Fresh whole chicken -1.8%, Non-prescription drugs -1.7%, Televisions -1.2%, Wine -0.8%, Prescription drugs -0.5%, Whole milk -0.2%. An italic note reads 'Note: CEA analysis of BLS data.' Overlaid in the foreground (lower left) is a cut-out photo of Trump in profile, in a dark navy suit, raising a clenched fist, wearing a red 'Gulf of America 45-47' cap, in front of a blurred American flag. The blue Department of Labor seal sits at lower right. Messaging technique ('THE TRUMP EFFECT' per the tweet): pairs an authoritative-looking falling-prices chart with a triumphant Trump pose to credit him for lower consumer prices. Target audience: cost-conscious voters/consumers.
notable_text: Prices for Many Goods and Services are Falling; Year-over-year price change; Eggs -39.2%; Note: CEA analysis of BLS data; (cap) Gulf of America 45-47; Department of Labor United States of America
